title: Today's Tech Brief: This Is How Trump Finally Signed the AI Executive Order
summary: A scaled-back AI executive order signed by Trump, Shai-Hulud malware infects Red Hat npm packages, and the tech world reacts.
imageAlt: Image from source article for This Is How Trump Finally Signed the AI Executive Order
imageCaption: This Is How Trump Finally Signed the AI Executive Order
imageSourceTitle: This Is How Trump Finally Signed the AI Executive Order

body.text: The AI Executive Order Finally Lands

President Donald Trump has signed a watered-down version of an executive order on AI. Initially shelved just two weeks ago, the order was revived after senior aides convinced him the administration couldn't indefinitely delay action on AI governance. The move signals a push to establish some boundaries around AI development and use, even if the order itself has been toned down. This development has been a long time coming, and it's interesting to see how the Trump administration is trying to balance innovation with regulation.
Malware Hits Red Hat npm Packages

Security researchers have spotted Shai-Hulud malware infecting Red Hat npm package versions that were downloaded around 80,000 times a week. This appears to be another example of malicious code sneaking into software supply chains. The culprit behind this malware – whether it's a copycat or the original TeamPCP – remains unclear. What's concerning is that this malware was hiding in plain sight, infecting packages that many developers trusted.
Another Week, Another AI Security Concern

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
